title: Creator credit in the viewer
question: How should the maker's name and links appear in the decision explorer?
rationale: Add a calm centered footer ('Made by Kayla Jones' + GitHub/LinkedIn/Website) under the list, with the links hardcoded. Because the viewer is one shared template (d28), the credit shows in every project's explorer for anyone who installs the skill - which is the intent: it's creator attribution for a tool being published. A footer was chosen over a top bar so it stays out of the way and fits the calm aesthetic.

tradeoffs.note: slim centered line with a hairline top border, out of the way; mirrors the README link row
static HTML/CSS in the shared template; nothing to plumb through the data
shared template (d28) means the maker's links show in every installer's explorer, not just their own projects - acceptable as attribution for a published tool
first thing seen, mirrors the README placement
adds a header the calm one-screen app deliberately doesn't have
each user sets their own author block; the maker's links show only in the maker's projects
new author schema in _project.json plus rendering plumbing through the generator and viewer
